content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=One-line role definitions for v3 memory components summary=Section "8. Модель данных в одной строке" provides a compact glossary mapping each core component to its role: `Source` (observed/said), `Entity` (what/who it is), `Note` (important but not yet cemented), `Claim` (normalized fact), `Profile` (compact stable-context projection), `Task` (to be done), `Episode` (what the system learned), and `MemoryRun` (how memory was updated). scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1>,entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6>,entity:<7>,entity:<8> keywords=one-line model,component roles,Source,Entity,Note,Claim,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun tags=doc_digest,data_model,glossary,v3 evidence=## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 Data Model layered architecture and cross-cutting concerns summary=The `v3` memory model is intentionally split into layers: evidence (`Source`), semantic graph (`Entity`, `Claim`), intermediate semantics (`Note`), operational projections (`Profile`, `Task`, `Episode`), and audit (`MemoryRun`), while applying two cross-cutting concerns: `scope` and `retrieval lifecycle`. This defines the core architectural direction and component roles. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3,data model,Source,Entity,Claim,Note,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun,scope,retrieval lifecycle tags=architecture,memory_model,layering useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` is an intermediate semantic memory layer with lifecycle and non-claim invariants summary=Section 3.4 defines `Note` as an intermediate semantic fragment (not source, not final claim, not chain-of-thought) used for retrievable but not yet cemented knowledge (e.g., decisions, hypotheses, plans, lessons, doc digests), with explicit lifecycle/status/maturity fields and invariants like one note per fragment and `candidate_claims_json` as hints only. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Note,semantic fragment,candidate_claims_json,status,retrieval_state,maturity_state tags=data_model,doc_digest,note_lifecycle useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` canonicalization and reversible merge policy via aliases and audit trail summary=In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes, with `entity_aliases` as a separate table for alias tracking. Invariants state that merges must preserve history, aliases must point to the current canonical entity, and wrong merges must be reversible through an audit trail. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,entity_aliases,canonical entity,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,entity_resolution,invariants,aliases useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim and Note have complementary roles and are not interchangeable summary=In 02. Data Model, `Claim` is for precise factual operations (factual answers, predicate-rule reconciliation, profile updates, and time-aware status), while `Note` is for richer contextual memory (decision history, rationale, evolving meaning, and later consolidation). Modeling everything as only one of them creates failure modes: false precision with all-`Claim` and overly soft memory with all-`N...[truncated 5 chars] scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Claim vs Note,factual answers,rationale tags=data_model,memory_roles,design_rationale useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`PredicateCatalog` defines reconciliation behavior per predicate, not a memory item summary=In 02. Data Model section 3.3, `PredicateCatalog` is specified as a reconciliation configuration layer where each predicate carries policies such as cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and projection sync flags (`profile_sync`, `task_sync`) to control how facts are merged and propagated. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.3 `PredicateCatalog`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy,profile_sync,task_sync tags=data_model,doc_digest,reconciliation_config useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Task memory is only for real future commitments summary=In 02. Data Model §3.7, `Task` is defined as operational memory for future actions and should be created only for explicit commitments, not for plain questions or requests. Each task must represent one clear obligation, and progress should be tracked by updating status instead of creating duplicates. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.7 `Task`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Task,future action,commitment,status update,duplicates tags=data_model,task,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`MemoryRun` is the audit/debug log layer for pipeline observability summary=In 02. Data Model §3.9, `MemoryRun` is defined as an audit trail and debug layer to trace prompt/model usage, retrieval contents, budget allocation, proposed vs applied ops, and pipeline failure points. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=MemoryRun,audit trail,debug layer,pipeline observability tags=data_model,memoryrun,doc_digest,observability useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Profile as compact rebuildable projection in 02. Data Model §3.6 summary=Section 3.6 positions `Profile` as a short, always-in-context projection layer built from explicit instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context, rather than source of truth. It should prioritize explicit facts over inference, keep disputed facts in Claim/Note, and remain rebuildable. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.6 `Profile`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Profile,projection layer,explicit facts,rebuildable,always-in-context tags=data_model,doc_digest,profile_invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<8> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`MemoryRun` recommended schema and invariants for durability/idempotency summary=Section §3.9 specifies a recommended `MemoryRun` field set (including `run_type`, `trigger_mode`, retrieval/prompt/model/ops/status/error/latency/token fields) and two key invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` supports idempotency and retry safety. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=run_type enum,trigger_mode,input_hash,retry safety,run log invariant tags=data_model,memoryrun,schema,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<9> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim model invariants and lifecycle in 02. Data Model §3.5 summary=Section 3.5 defines `Claim` as the atomic fact/relation unit for factual answers, predicate reconciliation, and explainable retrieval, with strict lifecycle/status fields and evidence linkage via `claim_sources`. Core rules include one predicate application per claim, self-contained meaning, normalization of relative time, and preferring `Note` when certainty is too soft. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.5 `Claim`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Claim,claim_sources,status,retrieval_state,predicate reconciliation tags=data_model,doc_digest,claim_invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<10> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Episode is a post-MVP lesson-oriented memory, not reasoning trace storage summary=In 02. Data Model §3.8, `Episode` is an optional memory type planned after MVP to preserve transferable experience such as successful tactics, failure patterns, and reusable lessons. Episodes must store distilled lessons and must not contain raw chain-of-thought or full reasoning traces. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.8 `Episode`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Episode,MVP,lessons,successful tactics,chain-of-thought tags=data_model,episode,memory_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<11> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 retention policy requires explicit lifecycle handling summary=Section 6 states that v3 must use an explicit retention/forgetting policy, including cooling low-use notes, archiving old resolved items, handling sensitive source data with requested soft/hard delete, and keeping historical claims explainable while lowering their retrieval prominence. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 6. Retention / forgetting' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=retention policy,cold,archived,soft delete,hard delete tags=data_model,retention,lifecycle useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<12> type=PLAN status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended Note maturity lifecycle and promotion/demotion signals summary=The section defines a maturity model for `Note` with stages `fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`, and operational signals for updates: usage in retrieval, support by multiple `Source`s, surviving time without supersede, and recurring rationale coverage increase maturity; contradiction, supersede, scope drift, and long-term disuse decrease or reset it. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 5. Note maturity' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Note maturity,fresh stabilizing mature consolidated,supersede tags=maturity_model,retrieval_governance,note_lifecycle useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<13> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Source` as immutable evidence layer with explicit lifecycle controls summary=In 02. Data Model section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw knowledge/evidence layer for chat/tool/imported/external records, intended for re-check and explainability. Its invariants require no silent mutation, separate `observed_at` from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce an explicit delete policy. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,source,invariants,schema useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<14> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Scope envelope and truth-vs-retrieval lifecycle separation in 02. Data Model summary=The document prescribes dual scope representation (`scope_text` for human explainability and `scope_json` for retrieval/reconciliation), and explicitly separates `truth status` from `retrieval_state` as independent axes. Retrieval lifecycle follows `active`, `warm`, `cold`, `archived`, `deleted`, with usage fields (`use_count`, `last_used_at`, `last_validated_at`) supporting maturity, retention, a...[truncated 10 chars] scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=scope_text,scope_json,truth status,retrieval_state,active,warm,cold,archived,deleted,use_count,last_used_at,last_validated_at tags=schema_pattern,retrieval,lifecycle,governance useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<15> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Retention metadata fields recommended for lifecycle traceability summary=The section recommends storing `retention_class`, `expires_at`, `deleted_at`, and `archived_at` as explicit fields to support retention and forgetting operations. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 6. Retention / forgetting' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=retention_class,expires_at,deleted_at,archived_at tags=schema,retention_fields,data_model useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

id=hot-path:note:<13> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Source` as immutable evidence layer with explicit lifecycle controls summary=In 02.
